reasoning.issue: The question asks what test the builder/available amount basket ('Cumulative Amount') is based on and when it begins to accrue.
reasoning.analysis: The Cumulative Amount is defined as the sum (without duplication) of multiple additive sources, all measured cumulatively from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs (growth_start_anchor: closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start across all source norms; start_date_language: 'the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs' per the builder_basket entity). The basket is NOT based on a single ratio test but rather is a cumulative accumulation basket. Its core earnings component uses a 'greatest of' structure among three alternative tests: (1) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income since the anchor date (6e76ed06_builder_source_cni, cap_grower_pct: 50%, ref: consolidated_net_income); (2) the Available Retained ECF Amount — the portion of cumulative Excess Cash Flow not required to prepay term loans under Section 2.10(f) or other indebtedness under Section 2.10(h), floored at $0 per fiscal year (6e76ed06_builder_source_ecf); (3) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period (6e76ed06_builder_source_ebitda_fc, cap_grower_pct: 140%, ref: consolidated_ebitda_ltm). These three feed into 6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate via a greatest_of aggregation function, which then contributes to the overall basket. Additionally, the basket is incremented (summed) by: a starter amount equal to the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA (6e76ed06_builder_source_starter); 100% of equity proceeds (6e76ed06_builder_source_other_equity_proceeds); retained asset sale proceeds after mandatory sweep (6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds); investment returns (6e76ed06_builder_source_investment_returns); and debt-to-equity conversion amounts (6e76ed06_builder_source_other_debt_conversion). The basket is reduced by usage for RPs, RDPs, and investments (reduced_by_rp_usage, reduced_by_rdp_usage, reduced_by_investment_usage all true). It is also shared with the builder RDP basket (Section 6.09(a)(A)).
reasoning.interactions: The three-test aggregate (6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate) uses a greatest_of aggregation and feeds into 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission alongside the additive sources (starter, equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, debt conversion). The builder basket shares capacity with the builder RDP basket (6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission, Section 6.09(a)(A)), meaning RDP usage reduces RP builder capacity and vice versa. Retained asset sale proceeds flow in via the proceeds_flow edge when asset sale proceeds are retained below sweep thresholds.
reasoning.conclusion: The Cumulative Amount basket is a cumulative accumulation basket (not a pure ratio test) that starts growing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs; its core earnings component uses the greatest of three tests: (i) 50% of cumulative CNI, (ii) the Available Retained ECF Amount, or (iii) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA, plus additive increments from a starter amount, equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt conversions.

stage2.answer: The build-up basket (defined as the 'Cumulative Amount') starts growing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32]. It is not a single-ratio-test basket; rather, it accumulates as the sum (without duplication) of: (1) a starter amount equal to the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA; (2) the greatest of (a) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income, (b) the Available Retained ECF Amount (cumulative excess cash flow not required to sweep term loans under Section 2.10(f), floored at $0), and (c) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA; plus (3) 100% of equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt-conversion amounts — all measured cumulatively since the anchor date [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32]. The basket is reduced dollar-for-dollar by usage for restricted payments, restricted debt payments (Section 6.09(a)(A), p.199), and investments.

greatest_of aggregation of CNI, ECF, and EBITDA-FC sources contributing to the Cumulative Amount
100% of equity_proceeds_usd, cumulative since closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start

investment returns, additive, cumulative since closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start
debt-to-equity conversion amounts, additive, cumulative since closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start
to the extent not prohibited by any applicable Intercreditor Agreement...with the Cumulative Amount

questions.gold_answer: The Cumulative Amount is based on the greatest of three tests: (1) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income (which amount shall not be less than zero for any fiscal quarter), (2) Excess Cash Flow not required to be applied to prepay Term Loans or any other debt (such amount cannot be less than $0 for any fiscal year), (3) cumulative Consolidated EBITDA minus 140% of cumulative Consolidated Fixed Charges. All tests start growing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower is permitted to distribute (by dividend or otherwise) equity interests it holds in Unrestricted Subsidiaries to its own equity holders or up the corporate chain.
reasoning.analysis: Section 6.06(p) contains an explicit categorical permission (norm_id: 6e76ed06_unrestricted_sub_equity_distribution_permission) for 'the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of, or Indebtedness owed to Holdings or a Restricted Subsidiary by, or any assets of, an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof.' The v3 entity (unsub_distribution_basket) confirms: is_categorical=true, covers_equity_interests=true, covers_indebtedness=true, covers_assets=true, covers_proceeds=true, partial_applicability=false, and capacity_category='categorical'. There are no dollar caps, no financial ratio conditions, and no defeaters identified in the extracted data. The permission also rebuilds the builder basket (rebuilds_builder_basket=true) and distributions can cascade (distributions_can_cascade=true). The J.Crew blocker (6e76ed06_jcrew_blocker_prohibition) restricts the designation of Restricted Subsidiaries as Unrestricted Subsidiaries if Material IP would be transferred, but does not restrict distributions of equity in already-designated Unrestricted Subsidiaries. The general RP basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) and other baskets are supplementary and not required for this specific permission. No defeaters defeat the Section 6.06(p) norm.
reasoning.interactions: The J.Crew blocker (6e76ed06_jcrew_blocker_prohibition) operates at the designation stage and does not defeat or condition the 6.06(p) distribution permission. The unsub_distribution_basket rebuilds the builder basket (Cumulative Amount), creating a positive interaction: distributions under 6.06(p) can replenish capacity for other restricted payments. The general RP basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) is a separate, independent permission and is not load-bearing here.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes, the Borrower is categorically permitted under Section 6.06(p) to distribute, by dividend or otherwise, equity interests it holds in Unrestricted Subsidiaries, without any dollar cap, ratio condition, or other limitation identified in the extracted data.

stage2.answer: Yes. Section 6.06(p) [p. 198] grants a categorical, uncapped permission for 'the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of, or Indebtedness owed to Holdings or a Restricted Subsidiary by, or any assets of, an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof.' This basket is unconditional—no financial ratio test, no dollar cap, and no defeaters apply to it. Notably, such distributions also rebuild the Cumulative Amount (builder basket), and distributions can cascade. The J.Crew blocker (Definition of Unrestricted Subsidiary, p. 89) applies only at the designation stage and does not restrict distributions of equity in already-designated Unrestricted Subsidiaries.

reasoning.issue: The question asks whether any investment baskets, restricted debt payment (RDP) baskets, or other baskets can be reallocated and used to make restricted payments or dividends.
reasoning.analysis:
Four key reallocation mechanisms are present in the data:

1. GENERAL RP BASKET (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission, §6.06(j)): A general-purpose basket capped at the greater of $130M / 100% Consolidated EBITDA permits dividends. Its action_scope is 'reallocable' and capacity_composition is 'fungible', confirming it can be used across covenant categories.

2. GENERAL RDP BASKET (6e76ed06_general_rdp_basket_permission, §6.09(a)(I)): Also capped at greater of $130M / 100% Consolidated EBITDA, this basket permits prepayments/redemptions of subordinated debt. Its action_scope is also 'reallocable'. Critically, the investment_pathway entity at §6.03(y) expressly provides that the general investment basket equals the greater of $130M/100% EBITDA PLUS the aggregate total of all amounts available under the RDP basket at §6.09(a) — confirming the RDP basket capacity is readable cross-covenant into investments. The converse reallocation (RDP basket capacity used for RPs) is also structurally possible given the parallel basket sizes and fungible design.

3. BUILDER / CUMULATIVE AMOUNT (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission, §Definition of Cumulative Amount): This is explicitly a shared pool used for restricted payments, RDPs, AND investments — reduced_by_rp_usage=true, reduced_by_rdp_usage=true, reduced_by_investment_usage=true. Usage under any one category reduces the pool available for the others. The builder_rdp_basket (6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission, §6.09(a)(A)) draws on the same Cumulative Amount as the RP builder basket (shares_with_rp_builder=true), meaning the Cumulative Amount is a single shared pool across RP, RDP, and investment uses.

4. INVESTMENT BASKET CROSS-REFERENCE: The §6.03(y) investment pathway explicitly incorporates the full RDP basket capacity at §6.09(a) as additive capacity for investments, establishing a direct cross-covenant reallocation link from RDP to investments. While the data does not show an explicit provision allowing unused investment basket capacity to flow back into RP dividends, the fungible/reallocable attributes on the general RP and RDP baskets, and the shared Cumulative Amount pool, are the primary reallocation mechanisms.
reasoning.interactions: The builder_rdp_basket (6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission) and the builder_usage_permission (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) share the same Cumulative Amount pool (shares_with_rp_builder=true), so usage for RDPs reduces availability for RPs and vice versa. The general_rdp_basket (6e76ed06_general_rdp_basket_permission) is cross-referenced in investment pathway §6.03(y), which adds RDP capacity to the investment basket cap. The general_rp_basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) has fungible capacity_composition and reallocable action_scope. No defeaters are present in the data.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) is a single shared pool used for RPs, RDPs, and investments, and the general RDP basket at §6.09(a)(I) is explicitly cross-referenced into the investment basket at §6.03(y); the general RP basket at §6.06(j) and general RDP basket at §6.09(a)(I) carry identical caps and reallocable action scopes.

stage2.answer: Yes, there are multiple cross-covenant reallocation pathways. First, the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) is explicitly a single shared pool [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32] that reduces dollar-for-dollar across restricted payments, restricted debt payments, and investments — confirmed by reduced_by_rp_usage, reduced_by_rdp_usage, and reduced_by_investment_usage all being true; the builder RDP basket at §6.09(a)(A) [p.199] draws on the same Cumulative Amount as the RP builder basket (shares_with_rp_builder=true), subject to any applicable intercreditor or subordination terms. Second, the general RDP basket at §6.09(a)(I) [p.200] (greater of $130M / 100% Consolidated EBITDA, reallocable) is expressly incorporated by reference into the general investment basket at §6.03(y), which caps general investments at the greater of $130M/100% EBITDA plus the full amount available under §6.09(a) — establishing a direct RDP-to-investment reallocation link. Third, the general RP basket at §6.06(j) [p.196] carries an identical cap (greater of $130M / 100% Consolidated EBITDA), fungible capacity_composition, and reallocable action_scope, confirming it operates in the same capacity pool as the general RDP basket. Collectively, amounts consumed under investments or RDPs reduce the Cumulative Amount available for dividends, and the general baskets for RPs and RDPs are structurally parallel and reallocable.

reasoning.issue: Whether asset sale proceeds can be used to fund dividends, considering the mandatory sweep obligations, sweep exemptions, and the builder basket's retained-asset-sale-proceeds source.
reasoning.analysis: The agreement establishes a mandatory prepayment sweep of Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales under Section 2.10(c), with the percentage determined by the First Lien Leverage Ratio: (a) 100% sweep if leverage > 5.75x [norm: 6e76ed06_sweep_tier_100pct, p.5]; (b) 50% sweep if leverage > 5.50x but ≤ 5.75x [norm: 6e76ed06_sweep_tier_50pct, p.5]; (c) 0% sweep if leverage ≤ 5.50x [norm: 6e76ed06_sweep_tier_0pct, p.6]. Accordingly, when leverage is ≤ 5.50x, no proceeds are swept to prepay loans and all proceeds are 'retained.' When leverage is between 5.50x–5.75x, 50% is retained. When leverage is > 5.75x, 100% must be prepaid and nothing is retained (absent an exemption). Additionally, a product-line exemption at Section 2.10(c)(iv) disapplies the sweep entirely for dispositions of all or substantially all of a product line or line of business, provided First Lien Leverage on a pro forma basis is ≤ the greater of 6.25x and the current ratio [norm: 6e76ed06_sweep_exemption_product_line, p.106]. Net Cash Proceeds that are retained (i.e., not required to be swept) flow into clause (f) of the Cumulative Amount definition as 'retained asset sale proceeds' [norm: 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds, p.32; proceeds_flow: retained_after_sweep]. The Cumulative Amount (builder basket) explicitly scopes make_dividend_payment as a permitted use [norm: 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission]. Therefore, asset sale proceeds retained after any applicable sweep can be used to make dividends through the builder basket.
reasoning.interactions: The sweep tier norms (6e76ed06_sweep_tier_100pct, _50pct, _0pct) determine how much of the Net Cash Proceeds must be prepaid versus retained. Only proceeds that are not required to be swept flow into 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds via the proceeds_flow edge (proceeds_flow_kind: retained_after_sweep). That source contributes (child_index 6, aggregation: sum) to 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission, which permits dividends. The sweep_exemption_product_line norm can exempt an entire asset sale from sweep, making 100% of proceeds potentially available as retained proceeds for the builder basket. No defeaters are present.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes, asset sale proceeds that are not required to be swept to prepay loans (i.e., retained proceeds) flow into the Cumulative Amount builder basket and may be used to make dividends; the retained amount depends on the leverage-based sweep tier applicable at the time of the sale.

stage2.answer: Yes, but only to the extent the proceeds are not required to be swept under Section 2.10(c). The sweep percentage is 100% if First Lien Leverage Ratio exceeds 5.75x [Definition of Applicable Net Cash Proceeds Percentage, clause (a), p.5], 50% if leverage is between 5.50x–5.75x [clause (b), p.5], and 0% if leverage is ≤ 5.50x [clause (c), p.6]. Proceeds retained after the applicable sweep (or exempt from sweep entirely, e.g., under the product-line exemption in Section 2.10(c)(iv) if leverage ≤ the greater of 6.25x and the current ratio) flow into clause (f) of the Cumulative Amount definition [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32], which expressly permits use for dividends via the builder basket.

reasoning.issue: The question asks for the total quantifiable dividend capacity across all applicable baskets in the restricted payments covenant, categorized and summed appropriately.
reasoning.analysis:
Per synthesis_guidance for Category N, baskets must be classified by capacity_category and aggregated only within the correct category.

**1. UNLIMITED CONDITIONAL basket (ratio test):**
- `6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission` [§6.06(o)]: Unlimited dividends if, on a pro forma basis, First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x OR no-worse test is satisfied. No fixed dollar cap — not quantifiable as a dollar figure. Listed separately.

**2. GENERAL PURPOSE baskets (summed):**
- `6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission` [§6.06(j)]: greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA (LTM). capacity_composition = 'fungible' — this basket's capacity is fungible (shared pool with any basket it reallocates to/from), but no shares_capacity_pool link to the builder basket is present in the data, so it is treated as independent. Fixed dollar floor: $130,000,000.
- `6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission` [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32]: The Cumulative Amount is general_purpose and computed from multiple sources. The fixed starter component is the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA (starter_dollar_amount = $130M, starter_ebitda_pct = 100%). Additional sources (50% CNI, retained ECF, 140% EBITDA vs. ECF vs. CNI greatest-of component, 100% equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, debt conversions) are variable and not quantifiable without current financial data. The builder is also shared with the RDP builder basket (shares_with_rp_builder = true) and is reduced by RDP and investment usage — meaning it is a shared pool across RP, RDP, and investment covenants. Fixed floor from starter alone: $130,000,000 (before reductions).
- `6e76ed06_general_rdp_basket_permission` [§6.09(a)(I)]: This is an RDP basket (capacity_category = general_purpose), not an RP basket. It is a separate covenant hierarchy. Per guidance, RDP baskets are separate — do NOT sum into the RP dividend total. Noted separately.

For the two general_purpose RP baskets (§6.06(j) and Cumulative Amount): no shares_capacity_pool link exists between them in the data, so each is independent capacity. Fixed dollar floor sum = $130M (§6.06(j)) + $130M (Cumulative Amount starter) = **$260,000,000 minimum fixed dollar capacity** (each also grows with 100% of Consolidated EBITDA, so the grower component could exceed $130M and would govern in that case).

**3. RESTRICTED PURPOSE baskets (listed separately, not summed into general total):**
- `6e76ed06_management_equity_basket_permission` [§6.06(b)]: greater of $20,000,000 and 15% of Consolidated EBITDA per annum; carryforward permitted; restricted to equity repurchases for management/employees.
- `6e76ed06_tax_distribution_basket_permission` [§6.06(c)]: categorical — no fixed dollar cap; limited to standalone-taxpayer-equivalent tax amounts.
- Holdco overhead basket [§6.06(k)]: categorical, no fixed dollar cap, restricted to overhead/public company costs.
- Equity award basket [§6.06(d)]: categorical/unlimited on condition, restricted to cashless exercise and tax withholding repurchases.

**4. CATEGORICAL baskets (listed separately):**
- `6e76ed06_unrestricted_sub_equity_distribution_permission` [§6.06(p)]: categorical — permits distribution of equity interests, indebtedness, or assets of Unrestricted Subsidiaries (or proceeds). No dollar cap; value depends on unsub assets at time of distribution.

**General RDP basket note:** `6e76ed06_general_rdp_basket_permission` is a general_purpose basket under the RDP covenant [§6.09(a)(I)] — greater of $130M and 100% Consolidated EBITDA — but it governs restricted debt payments, not dividends directly. Noted for completeness but excluded from dividend capacity total.
reasoning.interactions: The builder basket (Cumulative Amount) is shared with the RDP builder basket (builder_rdp_basket, shares_with_rp_builder = true) and reduced by RDP and investment usage (reduced_by_rdp_usage = true, reduced_by_investment_usage = true), meaning the $130M starter floor for the builder could be reduced by prior draws on those baskets. The general_rp_basket [§6.06(j)] has capacity_composition = 'fungible' but no explicit shares_capacity_pool link to the builder basket is present, so the two are counted independently per guidance. The ratio basket [§6.06(o)] is unlimited if the leverage test is met, making it the dominant capacity source when leverage is at or below 5.75x (or no-worse), but it is not quantifiable in dollar terms.
reasoning.conclusion: The total quantifiable fixed-dollar dividend capacity floor from general_purpose RP baskets is at minimum $260,000,000 ($130M under §6.06(j) + $130M starter under the Cumulative Amount), each also growing with 100% of Consolidated EBITDA LTM if that exceeds the floor. An additional restricted-purpose management equity basket provides up to the greater of $20M or 15% of Consolidated EBITDA annually. The ratio basket at §6.06(o) provides unlimited capacity if First Lien Leverage Ratio is ≤5.75x pro forma (or no-worse), but this is not a fixed dollar figure.

stage2.answer: The total quantifiable dividend capacity consists of: (1) **General-purpose RP baskets — minimum $260,000,000 fixed floor** (each growing with 100% of Consolidated EBITDA LTM if larger): (a) §6.06(j) general basket — greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA [Section 6.06(j), p.196]; and (b) the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) — starter component of greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA, plus variable upward sources (50% cumulative CNI, retained ECF, 140% EBITDA vs. ECF/CNI greatest-of test, 100% equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, and investment returns) [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32] — but note the builder is shared with and reduced by RDP and investment basket usage. (2) **Restricted-purpose basket** — management equity repurchase basket at the greater of $20,000,000 and 15% of Consolidated EBITDA per annum (with carryforward), usable only for management/employee equity repurchases [Section 6.06(b), p.195]. (3) **Unlimited conditional basket** — unlimited dividends if, pro forma, First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤5.75x (or the no-worse test is satisfied) [Section 6.06(o), p.198]; this is not a fixed dollar amount. (4) **Categorical/non-quantifiable baskets** — tax distributions (standalone-taxpayer equivalent, §6.06(c)), holdco overhead (§6.06(k)), cashless equity award repurchases (§6.06(d)), and distributions of Unrestricted Subsidiary equity/assets (§6.06(p)) — none carry a fixed dollar cap and are excluded from the dollar total.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower can dividend a $200m asset/business division (with negative EBITDA) to shareholders when the First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 6.0x, using the ratio-based RP basket under Section 6.06(o).
reasoning.analysis:
The primary basket to analyze is the ratio RP basket under Section 6.06(o) (norm_id: 6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission). This basket permits unlimited Dividends — including dividends of a business_division (expressly scoped as an object) — on a pro forma basis, subject to the condition that the First Lien Leverage Ratio is no greater than the GREATER OF: (x) 5.75x, OR (y) the First Lien Leverage Ratio computed WITHOUT giving effect to the subject transactions (i.e., the 'no-worse' test).

Pro forma analysis: The business division has $200m of assets but NEGATIVE EBITDA. Removing an asset/division with negative EBITDA from the consolidated group INCREASES consolidated EBITDA (because negative EBITDA is subtracted from the denominator, and removing it improves the ratio). Therefore, on a pro forma basis after the dividend, the First Lien Net Leverage Ratio will be LOWER than the current 6.0x pre-transaction ratio.

The no-worse test (condition predicate: pro_forma_no_worse|||first_lien_net_leverage) is the operative prong here: if pro forma leverage after the transaction is no worse than (i.e., equal to or better than) pre-transaction leverage of 6.0x, the condition is satisfied. Since disposing of a negative-EBITDA asset improves leverage, the pro forma ratio will be below 6.0x — which is better than the pre-transaction 6.0x. The no-worse test is therefore SATISFIED even though the absolute 5.75x threshold is not met.

The general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) (norm_id: 6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) provides capacity of the greater of $130m and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA — this is a separate, independent general-purpose basket, but the value of the division ($200m) exceeds the dollar floor. Whether EBITDA-grower capacity covers $200m depends on consolidated EBITDA, which is not provided. However, because the ratio basket (6.06(o)) is satisfied via the no-worse test, this basket need not bear the load.

The unsub distribution basket (6.06(p)) is categorical and covers assets of Unrestricted Subsidiaries — it does not appear directly applicable unless the asset/division is first designated as an Unrestricted Subsidiary, which involves a separate $40m investment cap and J.Crew blocker constraints on IP.

Conclusion: The ratio basket under 6.06(o) permits the dividend via the no-worse test because removing negative-EBITDA assets improves pro forma leverage below the current 6.0x.
reasoning.interactions: The ratio basket (6.06(o)) and general RP basket (6.06(j)) are independent capacity sources — no shares_capacity_pool link exists between them, so each provides separate capacity. No defeaters are present in the data. The scope_edges of 6.06(o) expressly include business_division as an object, confirming non-cash dividends of divisions are covered. The no-worse test (condition_root_1) operates as an OR alternative to the 5.75x absolute test (condition_root_0), meaning satisfaction of EITHER prong is sufficient.
reasoning.conclusion: The Borrower can dividend the $200m negative-EBITDA business division to shareholders at 6.0x leverage because the Section 6.06(o) ratio basket is satisfied via the no-worse test: removing a negative-EBITDA asset improves pro forma leverage below the pre-transaction 6.0x, satisfying the 'no greater than pre-transaction leverage' prong of the OR condition.

stage2.answer: Yes, the Borrower can dividend the $200m business division to shareholders at 6.0x First Lien Net Leverage, using the unlimited ratio RP basket under Section 6.06(o) [p. 198]. That basket requires pro forma First Lien Leverage Ratio to be no greater than the GREATER OF (x) 5.75x or (y) the pre-transaction ratio — an OR condition. At 6.0x pre-transaction, the absolute 5.75x threshold is not met, but the no-worse test (prong (y)) is the operative alternative: because the business division carries negative EBITDA, dividing it out of the consolidated group removes negative EBITDA from the denominator, thereby IMPROVING the pro forma leverage ratio below 6.0x — which is no worse than the pre-transaction 6.0x, satisfying prong (y). The basket expressly scopes business_division as a permissible object of a dividend, so a non-cash dividend of the division itself (not just cash proceeds) falls within the basket's action scope. Separately, the general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) [p. 196] provides independent capacity of the greater of $130m and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA, though the $200m asset value may exceed the dollar floor and the EBITDA grower would need to be calculated; however, this basket is not needed given the ratio basket is available.
